images.caption:
Figure 2:Closed-form admissible cross-entropy step on a binary belief slice.
Panel A shows the raw curvature endpointηmaxCE(p)=2mini(pi)2/maxi(pi)\eta_{\max}^{\mathrm{CE}}(p)=2\min_{i}(p_{i})^{2}/\max_{i}(p_{i}),
which shrinks rapidly as the belief approaches the simplex boundary.
Panel B shows that the ADS entropy barrier does not change the endpoint;
it supplies a multiplicative backoff from it.
Panel C visualizes the resulting certified region:
a chosen stepη\etais admissible only below the entropy-aware boundaryηCE​(B,p)\eta_{\mathrm{CE}}(B,p).
The figure makes the main claim operational: admissibility is a region in belief space, not a tuned scalar.

header: 10Diagnostic experiment: bound violation under distribution shift

images.caption: Figure 4:Belief-space distribution-shift experiment.A:KL divergence to the true target. Highη\eta(red) diverges after the shift; Low (green) and ADS-aware (blue) converge.B:Belief in the phase-2 target class. Highη\etacollapses to 0 (boundary); Low and ADS track the new target.C:Normalized belief entropy. The spike at the shift (dashed line) is visible in all strategies.D:Admissible boundηmax​(p)\eta_{\max}(p)(gray) vs. effective step size. Highη\etasits orders of magnitude above the bound throughout.E:Step-size ratioη/ηmax\eta/\eta_{\max}. For Highη\etathis ratio reaches>103>10^{3}after convergence.F:Belief trajectory on the probability simplex. Highη\etaspirals to the wrong vertex; Low and ADS follow the shift.
